sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Difficulty swallowing and a sensation of food getting stuck in the throat.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports a gradual onset of difficulty swallowing over the past four to five weeks, primarily with solid foods. He describes a sensation of food "sticking" in his throat and occasional discomfort, particularly when chewing solid foods like sandwiches. He has not experienced any significant choking episodes but reports a feeling of needing to 'wash down' solid foods occasionally. He denies any recent trauma or significant changes in his diet. He is currently taking metformin 500 mg twice daily and reports feeling generally well. He checks his blood sugar approximately 80% of the time, usually in the morning, and the numbers are within his target range. He is taking his blood pressure medication as prescribed and reports occasional readings slightly above his target range at home.
Findings
medical_concepts: sensation of food getting stuck in the throat

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Physical exam reveals no neck swelling or masses. The thyroid is normal in palpation. The neck and oral cavity examination reveals no redness or mucosal abnormalities. Lung exam is clear. The heart exam is normal with no murmurs. The abdomen is soft and non-tender. There is some epigastric tenderness upon palpation. Neurological exam is intact. Blood pressure is 120/50 mmHg in the office. Hemoglobin A1c was 7.4% at the last visit. No nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea reported.
Assessment
The patient's symptoms of difficulty swallowing are most concerning for a potential esophageal narrowing or stricture, possibly secondary to gastroesophageal reflux. His history of diabetes and hypertension are relevant to his overall health management.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: I am ordering a gastroenterology referral for a scope to evaluate the esophagus and rule out stricture or other structural abnormalities. I am also prescribing prilocaine 20 mg twice daily for symptomatic relief. I recommend frequent chewing of solid foods and ensuring adequate hydration. I will order a repeat hemoglobin A1c and additional blood work, including a kidney function panel, to assess his diabetes control. I am also ordering a refill for his norvasc medication. I will schedule a follow-up appointment in one month to review the scope results and assess treatment response. I am also ordering an ophthalmology referral for a dilated eye exam to screen for diabetic retinopathy.

medical_concepts: sensation of food getting stuck in the throat
